name: Core Multiservices-Credit Repair
description_short: Core Multiservices offers credit repair by challenging Metro2 reporting compliance rather than disputing account validity, plus secured credit card building and mortgage readiness services.
description_long:
Core Multiservices Inc is a credit repair firm based in Jackson Heights, New York, founded by Mohammad Kashem. The company positions itself as offering an alternative approach to traditional credit repair, focusing on compliance challenges rather than validity disputes. According to their marketing, they serve consumers seeking to improve credit scores before major purchases like mortgages or automobiles.

The company's primary service is credit repair targeting negative items including late payments, collections, charge-offs, inquiries, foreclosures, garnishments, tax liens, judgments, bankruptcies, and child support obligations. They also offer credit report analysis, credit score optimization guidance, credit rebuilding assistance with new credit cards, mortgage readiness consultation (leveraging underwriting expertise), auto loan readiness with dealer partnerships, and a secured credit card product ($200 deposit, no credit check required) that reports to all three bureaus. A credit report service is offered at $1 for 7 days, then $21.99/month.

Core Multiservices distinguishes itself by claiming to challenge Metro2 reporting format compliance rather than disputing account validity, regardless of whether accounts are actually delinquent. They assert this methodology is used by fewer than 5% of credit repair firms nationwide and produces "faster, dependable, reliable and ethical results." The website emphasizes that traditional dispute methods are ineffective, citing statistics that 90% of consumers who dispute see no score change.

A critical assessment reveals significant concerns: the company's core claim—that disputing compliance rather than validity is legal and ethical—is presented without regulatory context or disclaimers. Their statistics about dispute ineffectiveness appear designed to justify their alternative approach but lack sourcing. No information about licensing, certifications, guarantees, or compliance with CROA (Credit Repair Organizations Act) is provided. The secured credit card offering appears to be a partnership rather than direct issuance, and pricing details beyond initial costs are incomplete.

In the broader ecosystem of credit repair services, consumers have multiple paths to improving their credit. Professional credit repair companies can dispute inaccurate items with all three bureaus, while credit monitoring services provide ongoing alerts about changes to your reports. For those building credit from scratch, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured approaches. Consumers dealing with overwhelming debt may benefit from debt consolidation loans to simplify payments, or credit counseling through nonprofit agencies for personalized budgeting guidance. Consumers who successfully repair their credit often find better rates on installment loans, secured credit cards, and other financial products.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Offers Metro2 compliance-based approach claimed to be used by less than 5% of firms
Provides comprehensive credit report analysis and identifies "delicate" dispute matters for review
Secured credit card product with no credit check required and $200 minimum deposit
Credit score optimization guidance focused on gaps and untapped opportunities in credit profiles
Mortgage readiness service backed by 10+ years of underwriting experience
Auto loan readiness with stated partnerships with auto dealers
Multi-service platform addressing credit repair, building, and financial readiness
Credit report access at promotional rate ($1 for 7 days)
cons: No mention of CROA compliance, licensing, certifications, or regulatory disclosures required for credit repair firms
Core methodology (Metro2 compliance challenges) lacks legal or regulatory context and sourcing
Success statistics (90% failure rate for traditional disputes) are presented without attribution or verification
No pricing transparency for credit repair services; only credit report pricing disclosed
No mention of consumer guarantees, performance warranties, or dispute success rates for their own services
Secured credit card appears to be partnership product, not directly issued; limited details on terms
best_for: Consumers with negative credit items seeking pre-mortgage approval preparation
Individuals seeking to build credit with a secured card and no credit check requirement
Those wanting credit score optimization guidance before auto or major purchase loans
Consumers skeptical of traditional credit dispute methods seeking alternative approaches
diagnosis: Core Multiservices is best for consumers seeking alternative credit repair methods before major purchases, particularly those interested in mortgage or auto financing readiness. However, the primary caveat is the complete absence of regulatory compliance documentation, licensing information, CROA disclaimers, and verified success metrics—critical transparency gaps for any credit repair firm.
services: Credit repair targeting late payments, collections, and charge-offs
Removal of foreclosures, garnishments, tax liens, and judgments from credit reports
Bankruptcy and child support notation removal
Credit report analysis and pro analysis of dispute candidates
Credit score optimization and gap identification
Credit card building and balance management guidance
Mortgage credit readiness consultation and preparation
Auto loan credit readiness with dealer partnerships
Secured credit card product ($200 deposit, no credit check, reports to all 3 bureaus)
Credit report access ($1 for 7 days, then $21.99/month)
Financial health assessment beyond credit score
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
meta_description: Core Multiservices offers credit repair by challenging Metro2 reporting compliance, plus secured credit card building and mortgage readiness services.
